name: GitHub Actions Executor
description: Use for compute-intensive, deterministic, or heavy-lifting tasks (e.g., running test suites, building images, data processing pipelines). Executes heavy-compute tasks via GitHub Actions. Requires a GitHub Token.

tools.name: executor-github.runWorkflow
tools.description: Creates and triggers a GitHub Actions workflow. Signature: runWorkflow({ workflowYaml: string, workflowName?: string, repoUrl?: string, branch?: string }). Pass the YAML content to this tool, and it will create the file and trigger the run. Returns the workflow run ID.

workflowYaml.description: The YAML content for the GitHub Actions workflow.

workflowName.description: A descriptive name for the workflow file (e.g., 'fleet-test-run.yml').

repoUrl.description: Optional. The repository URL (owner/repo) to run the workflow in. Defaults to the current context repository.

branch.description: Optional. The branch to run the workflow on. Defaults to the current context branch.

tools.name: executor-github.getRunStatus
tools.description: Checks the status of a previously triggered GitHub Actions workflow run. Signature: getRunStatus({ runId: number, repoUrl?: string }). Returns an object with status and conclusion.

runId.description: The ID of the workflow run to check.

repoUrl.description: Optional. The repository URL (owner/repo) where the workflow was run. Defaults to the current context repository.

tools.name: executor-github.fetchArtifacts
tools.description: Downloads artifacts from a completed GitHub Actions workflow run and saves them to the Fleet artifact store. Signature: fetchArtifacts({ runId: number, repoUrl?: string, branch?: string }).

runId.description: The ID of the workflow run to fetch artifacts from.

repoUrl.description: Optional. The repository URL (owner/repo) where the workflow was run. Defaults to the current context repository.

branch.description: Optional. The branch the workflow was run on. Defaults to the current context branch.
